### Query Breakdown

| Query | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|

### Notes
- **`:below` modifier** leverages FHIR terminology subsumption — requires the server to support hierarchical code lookups (e.g., via a FHIR Terminology Server).

### 🔴 Condition — Other & Unspecified Appendicitis (ICD-10-CM K36, K37)

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|80146002,http://snomed.info/sct|173171007&_include=Procedure:patient
```

---

### Code Reference

| Query | System | Code | Meaning |
|-------|--------|------|---------|
| Condition | SNOMED CT | `74400008` | Appendicitis (general) |
| Condition | SNOMED CT | `85189001` | Acute appendicitis |
| Condition | SNOMED CT | `44946007` | Chronic appendicitis |
| Condition | ICD-10-CM | `K35` + `:below` | All acute appendicitis subtypes (K35.2x, K35.3x, K35.8x, K35.9) |
| Condition | ICD-10-CM | `K36` | Other appendicitis |
| Condition | ICD-10-CM | `K37` | Unspecified appendicitis |
| Procedure | SNOMED CT | `80146002` | Appendectomy (open) |
| Procedure | SNOMED CT | `173171007` | Laparoscopic appendectomy |

### Design Notes
- **`:below` modifier** on K35 captures all granular subcodes (e.g., K35.20, K35.89) without enumerating each
- **Procedure search** acts as a **safety net** — patients who had an appendectomy may not have an explicit appendicitis diagnosis coded
- **`_include=:patient`** returns associated Patient resources in each bundle for downstream cohort analysis
- Deduplicate patient IDs across all four result sets to build the final population

## FHIR Queries: Appendicitis Patient Population

Two complementary queries are needed to capture the full population. Taking the **union** of patients returned by both ensures neither cohort is missed.

---

### Query 1 — Acute Appendicitis Diagnoses (Problem List)

Captures patients with a formal appendicitis diagnosis recorded as a problem-list item, using both SNOMED CT and ICD-10-CM codes across the full K35–K37 spectrum.

```
Condition?category=problem-list-item&code=http://snomed.info/sct|85189001,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.2,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.20,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.21,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.3,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.30,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.31,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.32,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.33,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.80,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K35.89,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K36,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|K37&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Query 2 — Appendectomy Procedures Performed

Captures patients who underwent an appendectomy (open or laparoscopic), which may be the **only** clinical record for patients sent directly to surgery without a separate diagnosis code.

```
Procedure?code=http://snomed.info/sct|80146002,http://snomed.info/sct|174041007,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|44950,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|44960,http://www.ama-assn.org/go/cpt|44970&status=completed&_include=Procedure:subject
```

---

## Code Reference Table

### Query 1 — Appendicitis Diagnosis Codes

| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `85189001` | Acute appendicitis |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.2` | Acute appendicitis with generalized peritonitis |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.20` | …without abscess |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.21` | …with abscess |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.3` | Acute appendicitis with localized peritonitis |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.30` | …without abscess |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.31` | …with perforation, without abscess |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.32` | …with perforation and abscess |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.33` | …with perforation and abscess, without peritonitis |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.80` | Other and unspecified acute appendicitis without abscess |
| ICD-10-CM | `K35.89` | Other acute appendicitis |
| ICD-10-CM | `K36` | Other appendicitis (chronic/recurrent) |
| ICD-10-CM | `K37` | Unspecified appendicitis |

### Query 2 — Appendectomy Procedure Codes

| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `80146002` | Appendectomy (open) |
| SNOMED CT | `174041007` | Laparoscopic appendectomy |
| CPT | `44950` | Appendectomy, open |
| CPT | `44960` | Appendectomy, open — ruptured appendix with abscess or generalized peritonitis |
| CPT | `44970` | Laparoscopic appendectomy |

---

## Population Logic

```
Final Study Population = 
  UNION(
    Patients from Query 1 [Condition.subject],
    Patients from Query 2 [Procedure.subject]
  )
```

> **Key design notes:**
> - **`category=problem-list-item`** in Query 1 anchors to the formal problem list; if your EHR also records encounter diagnoses separately, consider removing this filter or adding `category=encounter-diagnosis` as a third query.
> - **`status=completed`** in Query 2 excludes cancelled or entered-in-error procedures.
> - The **`_include=...:subject`** directives return the linked `Patient` resources in the same Bundle, enabling direct de-duplication by `Patient.id` across both result sets.

### Key Design Notes

| Consideration | Rationale |
|---|---|
| **No `clinical-status` filter** | Captures both active presentations and historically resolved (post-op) diagnoses |
